subject: Civics
overall_learning_objectives: Learn why a country needs a constitution
Know about the making of the Indian Constitution
Identify key features of the Indian Constitution
Understand the importance of Fundamental Rights
Recognize the values of equality, justice and secularism

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Constitution
topics_covered: Examples of rules in daily life
student_activities: Students list rules followed in school
Discuss why rules are important in society

lesson_plan.title: Why Does a Country Need a Constitution
student_activities: Students discuss what might happen if a country had no rules
Write two reasons why a constitution is important

lesson_plan.title: Making of the Indian Constitution
student_activities: Write the year when the Constitution came into effect
Discuss the role of leaders in drafting the Constitution

lesson_plan.title: Key Features of the Constitution
student_activities: Draw a simple diagram showing three branches of government

lesson_plan.title: Fundamental Rights
topics_covered: Examples of rights such as equality and freedom

lesson_plan.title: Values of the Constitution and Revision
student_activities: Students give examples of equality in daily life
Participate in a short revision quiz

teacher_tip: Use simple real-life examples such as school rules and rights to help students understand the importance of the Constitution.
